summary: The IDF Chief of Staff affirmed that the objective in southern Lebanon remains to defend northern Israeli communities, noting a fragile cease‑fire and recent killings of Hezbollah commanders. Iran’s parliamentary speaker and IRGC‑linked Tasnim warned of severe retaliation if Israel does not withdraw from Lebanon, linking it to the suspension of broader US‑Iran negotiations. The Home Front Command announced the lifting of civilian gathering restrictions in border towns as the cease‑fire holds.
key_points: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir said the military’s objective in southern Lebanon is unchanged—defending northern Israeli communities—and warned the cease‑fire is fragile (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf declared Iran will enter a phase of severe retaliation if Israel does not withdraw from Lebanon (Source: @shin_persian/7153).
The IDF Home Front Command will lift all gathering restrictions in communities near the Lebanon border starting at 6 a.m. tomorrow (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
Hezbollah commander Abu Muktapa, linked to Hamas operations, was killed in a precise Israeli strike in southern Gaza (Source: @New_security8200/142913).

Iran’s foreign ministry said technical teams will continue work after 18‑hour talks in Switzerland ended, with Qatar and Pakistan mediating a text on general understandings (Source: @lelotsenzura/93145).
US Secretary of State Antony Blinken will travel to the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain to discuss regional security and safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76583).
A senior Israeli official warned that if Israel retreats from the “yellow line,” Hezbollah’s threat to border communities will return (Source: @almogboker78/17556).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF Chief of Staff affirmed that the objective in southern Lebanon remains to defend northern Israeli communities, noting a fragile cease‑fire and recent killings of Hezbollah commanders. Iran’s parliamentary speaker warned of severe retaliation if Israel does not withdraw from Lebanon, tying it to a suspension of US‑Iran talks. The Home Front Command will lift civilian gathering limits in border towns as the cease‑fire holds.

points: Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf said Iran will enter a phase of severe retaliation if Israel does not withdraw from Lebanon, linking this to a halt in broader US‑Iran negotiations (Source: @shin_persian/7153).
Iranian Parliament speaker praised the national football team’s defence, comparing it to Iran’s historic defence against US‑Israeli aggression (Source: @presstv/195088).

Iran’s foreign ministry announced that after 18 hours of talks in Switzerland ended, technical teams will continue work, with Qatar and Pakistan mediating a text on general understandings (Source: @lelotsenzura/93145).
Iranian news agency Fars reported that Iran will not return to negotiations without a Trump apology and Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/62432).
Iranian agency Pars indicated uncertainty whether negotiations have ended, but the Iranian delegation appears close to returning home (Source: @asafroz/50584).

IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir stated the military’s objective in southern Lebanon is unchanged—defending northern Israeli communities—and warned the cease‑fire is fragile, with all resources allocated for rapid offensive action if needed (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
The IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Hamas operative Abu Muktapa, who participated in the kidnapping of Yagil Yaakov, during a strike in southern Gaza (Source: @New_security8200/142913).
The Home Front Command will lift all restrictions on gatherings in communities near the Lebanon border from 6 a.m. tomorrow, after a fresh assessment of the cease‑fire (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
A senior Israeli official warned that abandoning the “yellow line” would allow Hezbollah to resume threats against border settlements (Source: @almogboker78/17556).
Israeli air force conducted an assassination operation west of Gaza City, as reported by a local source (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372333).
Israeli air force carried out an assassination operation west of Gaza City (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372333).
Hamas operative Abu Muktapa, linked to the kidnapping of Yagil Yaakov, was killed in a precise Israeli strike in southern Gaza (Source: @New_security8200/142913).
IDF reported the elimination of a Hamas commander involved in training and recruitment activities within Gaza hospitals (Source: @New_security8200/142913).
Hezbollah’s mid‑level commanders have been killed in recent days, according to IDF Chief of Staff, indicating the group is in a “very difficult situation” (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
Israel’s chief of staff emphasized that activity in the Ali Taher and Beaufort areas of southern Lebanon serves the purpose of defending northern communities (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
The Home Front Command will lift all civilian gathering restrictions in border towns after a fresh assessment of the cease‑fire (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
A senior Israeli official warned that if Israel retreats from the “yellow line,” Hezbollah’s threat to border settlements will return (Source: @almogboker78/17556).
Iranian statements warned that if Israel does not withdraw from Lebanon, all negotiations with the United States will be suspended (Source: @shin_persian/7153).
US Secretary of State Antony Blinken will travel to the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain to discuss regional security and safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76583).
Qatar reported at least 54 injuries and 18 missing after an explosion at the Ras Laffan gas facility (Source: @asafroz/50588).
Iranian officials indicated that negotiations with the United States are close to resumption, with Qatar and Pakistan acting as mediators (Source: @lelotsenzura/93145).

key_developments: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir reaffirmed the unchanged objective of defending northern Israeli communities in southern Lebanon, noting a fragile cease‑fire and recent Hezbollah commander losses (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned of severe retaliation if Israel does not withdraw from Lebanon, linking it to a suspension of US‑Iran negotiations (Source: @shin_persian/7153).
The IDF Home Front Command will lift all civilian gathering restrictions in border towns near Lebanon starting at 6 a.m. tomorrow (Source: @manniefabian/58656).
Israeli air force conducted an assassination operation west of Gaza City (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372333).
Hamas operative Abu Muktapa, involved in the kidnapping of Yagil Yaakov, was killed in a precise Israeli strike in southern Gaza (Source: @New_security8200/142913).
US Secretary of State Antony Blinken will travel to the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain to discuss regional security and safe maritime passage (Source: @VahidOnline/76583).
Iran’s foreign ministry said technical teams will continue work after Swiss talks ended, with Qatar and Pakistan mediating a text on general understandings (Source: @lelotsenzura/93145).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran signals a willingness to resume severe retaliation if Israel maintains its presence in Lebanon, potentially expanding its proxy activities across the region. Hezbollah, despite recent commander losses, remains a potent threat to Israeli border communities, especially if cease‑fire violations occur. Israeli operations in Gaza and southern Lebanon indicate a high operational tempo, with targeted killings aimed at disrupting Hamas and Hezbollah networks, maintaining pressure on militant capabilities.
sections.regional_response: The United States is actively engaging Gulf partners, with Secretary of State Blinken’s upcoming visits to the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain focusing on maritime security and diplomatic channels to de‑escalate tensions. Qatar’s recent gas facility explosion underscores the vulnerability of critical infrastructure amid heightened regional alertness. Iran’s diplomatic overtures, mediated by Qatar and Pakistan, suggest a parallel track of negotiation despite hardline public statements, reflecting a complex interplay of military posturing and diplomatic outreach.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah commander movements in the Ali Taher and Beaufort sectors is critical, given recent Israeli strikes and reported command disruptions. Continued surveillance of Iranian IRGC communications is required to assess the likelihood of broader retaliation linked to the Lebanon stalemate. The elimination of Abu Muktapa highlights Israeli intelligence penetration within Hamas training cells in Gaza, suggesting further high‑value target opportunities. Gulf state security apparatuses should prepare for potential spill‑over effects from any escalation between Israel and Iran.
